sections.content:
Follow these instructions:

  • In the CloudShare environment console, locate the DevServer VM.
  • Click the Connect button (RDP or Console).
  • Log in using the provided Administrator credentials (found in the Credentials Tab).
sections.title: Section 1.1 - Connect to the Primary DevServer

sections.content:
This step ensures the DevServer can communicate with the backend database machine.

  • On DevServer, open Command Prompt or PowerShell.
  • Attempt to ping the database server by its hostname: 
    ping DBC-01
  • You should receive a successful reply. If you do not, check that both VMs are running.
sections.title: Section 1.2 - Verify Network PING

sections.content:
We require Node.js v18 for the starter project.

Follow these instructions:

  • Open the pre-installed web browser on DevServer.
  • Navigate to the official Node.js download page and download the LTS Windows Installer (.msi).
  • Run the installer, accepting all default settings.
  • Verify the installation in PowerShell: 
    node -v
npm -v
    Expected output: $v18.x.x$ and $9.x.x$
sections.title: Section 2.1 - Install Node.js Dependency

sections.content:
• Open Git Bash or PowerShell.
  • Navigate to the working directory:
    cd C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop
  • Clone the project repository:
    git clone [https://github.com/your-org/starter-app.git](https://github.com/your-org/starter-app.git)
  • Navigate into the new directory and install dependencies:
    cd starter-app
npm install
sections.title: Section 2.2 - Clone and Install Project

sections.content:
Your environment is successfully configured.

  • Journey Complete! You have finished the initial setup.
  • Next Task: Please proceed to the "Building the API" Experience.
sections.title: Section 3.1 - finish up
